Title: Molecular biodiversity of mycotoxigenic fungi that threaten food safety

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TRI12 is a core TRI-cluster transporter gene associated with trichothecene biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI12 is part of the 12-gene core TRI cluster. 2. It encodes a transporter. 3. It is associated with the trichothecene biosynthetic system.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific transport substrate/function not detailed in this review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TRI101 is a trichothecene-associated acyltransferase locus whose genomic location varies among Fusarium species and informs cluster evolution.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI101 occurs as a separate locus in F. graminearum and F. sporotrichioides but is present within the core TRI cluster in members of the F. incarnatum–F. equiseti species complex. 2. Its distribution supports relocation into the cluster during evolution. 3. It contributes to understanding TRI-cluster diversification.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Emphasized for evolutionary relocation rather than direct chemotype determination in this review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TRI3 is a core trichothecene biosynthetic gene that remains functional across 3-ADON, 15-ADON, and NIV chemotypes examined.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI3 encodes a trichothecene C-15 acetyltransferase. 2. It was functional in all three chemotypes analyzed. 3. Therefore chemotype differences between 3-ADON and 15-ADON are attributed to TRI8 rather than TRI3.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Useful as a non-causal comparator for chemotype differentiation.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Fusarium verticillioides; Fusarium oxysporum; Fusarium proliferatum
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Fusarium verticillioides; Fusarium oxysporum; Fusarium proliferatum
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FUM1 is the fumonisin polyketide synthase gene and serves as a key marker of fumonisin biosynthetic capacity and evolutionary history.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUM1 is a central fumonisin cluster gene present in fumonisin producers. 2. Phylogenetic relationships of FUM1 do not fully mirror species phylogeny. 3. The gene is useful for inferring cluster evolution and distribution.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review does not detail its catalytic step here, focusing instead on comparative cluster evolution.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: fum19 is frequently detected in A. niger isolates and, together with fum1, marks presence of at least part of the fum cluster in black aspergilli.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fum19 was detected in most surveyed A. niger isolates from raisins. 2. In nonproducing A. awamori only fum1 and fum19 were detected among eight fum genes tested. 3. These patterns suggest partial cluster deletion or strong sequence divergence in some nonproducing isolates.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific molecular function of fum19 is not described in the provided text.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CPM1 is a candidate gene adjacent to the FUM cluster that may underlie C-3 hydroxylation of some FC analogues, but its role is unconfirmed.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPM1 lies adjacent to the known FUM cluster in F. oxysporum. 2. It could be responsible for the extra C-3 hydroxylation seen in hydroxy-FC1 and iso-hydroxy-FC1. 3. Its role in fumonisin biosynthesis has not been determined.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate only; should be treated as unvalidated.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trichothecene intermediates; in F. sporotrichioides at C-8, and in F. graminearum at both C-7 and C-8.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly modifies core trichothecene scaffold toward final chemotype
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: type A versus type B trichothecene structural diversification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TRI1 functional polymorphism determines different oxygenation patterns and contributes to structural differences between type A trichothecenes such as T-2 toxin and type B trichothecenes such as DON.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: functional analyses; comparative sequence analysis; pathway comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI1 encodes a cytochrome P450 monooxygenase in trichothecene biosynthesis. 2. In F. sporotrichioides it hydroxylates at C-8, whereas in F. graminearum it hydroxylates at both C-7 and C-8. 3. This functional divergence contributes to major trichothecene chemotype differences.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its phylogeny is incongruent with species phylogeny in some Fusarium, suggesting distinct evolutionary history and relocation in some lineages.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes esterification of isovaleryl to the C-8 oxygen of trichothecene intermediates in F. sporotrichioides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional TRI16 promotes formation of type A trichothecenes such as T-2 toxin; nonfunctional TRI16 in F. graminearum contributes to type B trichothecene formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI16 encodes an acyltransferase required for C-8 isovaleryl ester formation in F. sporotrichioides. 2. TRI16 is nonfunctional in F. graminearum. 3. Functional state of TRI16 contributes to type A versus type B trichothecene differences.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogeny parallels TRI1 rather than core TRI genes in some species.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces the C-4 hydroxyl in type B trichothecenes.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C-4 hydroxylated trichothecene intermediate leading to NIV
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional TRI13 leads to NIV production, whereas nonfunctional TRI13 with deletions leads to DON production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: sequence polymorphism analysis; comparative chemotype association
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI13 polymorphism controls presence or absence of the C-4 hydroxyl. 2. Functional TRI13 is associated with NIV producers. 3. Deletion/nonfunctional TRI13 is associated with DON producers.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Similar deletion patterns occur in both F. graminearum and F. culmorum.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deacetylates 3,15-diacetyldeoxynivalenol at either C-15 or C-3 depending on allele type.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Differential Tri8 activity determines whether Fusarium strains produce 3-ADON or 15-ADON.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI8 coding polymorphism distinguishes 3-ADON and 15-ADON strains. 2. Tri8 from 3-ADON strains removes the C-15 acetyl, yielding 3-ADON. 3. Tri8 from 15-ADON/NIV strains removes the C-3 acetyl, yielding 15-ADON-type products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TRI3 remained functional in all three chemotypes examined.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes condensation of an amino acid with a linear polyketide backbone; with alanine forms the 20-carbon FB backbone, with glycine forms the 19-carbon FC backbone.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: alanine or glycine plus linear polyketide
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 20-carbon FB backbone or 19-carbon FC backbone intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms backbone of terminal fumonisin class
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FUM8 determines whether Fusarium produces B-series fumonisins or C-series fumonisins by selecting alanine versus glycine for backbone assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: precursor feeding studies; molecular genetic analyses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUM8 is a major determinant of FB versus FC production. 2. In FB biosynthesis Fum8 condenses alanine with an 18-carbon polyketide to form a 20-carbon backbone. 3. In FC biosynthesis Fum8 uses glycine to form a 19-carbon backbone.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogeny of FUM8 may be discordant with species phylogeny.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-10 hydroxylation of fumonisin intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: hydroxylation pattern branch leading to FB3/FB1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FUM2 contributes to fumonisin analogue diversity by installing the C-10 hydroxyl.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene inactivation; precursor feeding; biochemical analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUM2 encodes a cytochrome P450 monooxygenase. 2. It catalyzes C-10 hydroxylation. 3. Along with FUM3 it contributes to structural diversification among fumonisin analogues.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence supports two pathway branches in fumonisin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-5 hydroxylation of fumonisin intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: hydroxylation pattern branch leading to FB2/FB1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FUM3 contributes to fumonisin analogue diversity by installing the C-5 hydroxyl.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene inactivation; precursor feeding; biochemical analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUM3 encodes a dioxygenase. 2. It catalyzes C-5 hydroxylation. 3. Together with FUM2 it helps generate distinct hydroxylation patterns of FB analogues.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of fum8 is associated with fumonisin biosynthetic potential in some A. niger isolates, but gene presence alone does not reliably predict production because some nonproducing isolates retain fum genes.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fum8 was detected in a subset of A. niger isolates from grapes. 2. Most but not all fum8-positive isolates produced FB2 under the conditions tested. 3. fum gene presence alone is insufficient to predict production in black aspergilli.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses this mainly as a marker within a review context rather than a detailed functional characterization in Aspergillus.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: fum1 occurrence serves as a marker of fumonisin gene cluster presence in black aspergilli, but its presence alone does not guarantee toxin production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fum1 was detected in many A. niger isolates from raisins. 2. Some isolates carrying fum genes were nonproducing. 3. Structural or regulatory defects may explain loss of production despite gene presence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as part of cluster distribution rather than mechanistic enzyme study in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence/amplification of aflR is associated with aflatoxin-producing genotypes; absence of detectable aflR in some isolates is associated with nonproduction.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. aflR is one of two regulatory genes surveyed in A. flavus isolates. 2. All aflatoxin-producing isolates yielded amplification products for aflR. 3. Some nonproducing isolates lacked detectable aflR amplification, consistent with cluster variation or deletion.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this review, aflR is treated mainly as a diagnostic/regulatory cluster gene rather than experimentally dissected.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence/amplification of aflS is associated with aflatoxin-producing genotypes; absence of detectable aflS in some isolates is associated with nonproduction.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. aflS was surveyed together with aflR and structural afl genes. 2. Aflatoxin-producing isolates retained detectable aflS. 3. Nonproducing isolates often lacked amplification of aflS, indicating afl-cluster variability.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not provide deeper mechanistic analysis of aflS regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TRI6 is part of the core TRI cluster and is identified as a regulatory gene controlling trichothecene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI6 is one of the regulatory genes in the core TRI cluster. 2. It is conserved in the characterized core cluster of F. graminearum and F. sporotrichioides. 3. It is implicated in regulation of trichothecene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No new mechanistic findings are presented in this review.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TRI10 is part of the core TRI cluster and is identified as a regulatory gene involved in trichothecene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI10 is a regulatory gene in the core TRI cluster. 2. It is conserved in the characterized clusters of F. graminearum and F. sporotrichioides. 3. It contributes to regulation of trichothecene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No detailed mechanism is discussed in this review.
